8:["$","div",null,{"className":"bg-white dark:bg-stone-900 rounded-2xl p-6 sm:p-8 mt-4 shadow-lg border border-stone-100 dark:border-stone-800 mb-10","children":[["$","h1",null,{"className":"text-2xl sm:text-3xl lg:text-4xl font-bold text-stone-900 dark:text-stone-100 leading-tight mb-5","style":{"fontFamily":"'Playfair Display', Georgia, serif"},"children":"Morning Routine for Productivity: The Evidence-Based Approach"}],["$","div",null,{"className":"flex flex-wrap items-center gap-4 text-sm text-stone-400","children":[["$","$L3",null,{"href":"/authors/sara-osei","className":"flex items-center gap-2 group","children":[["$","img",null,{"src":"https://images.unsplash.com/photo-1438761681033-6461ffad8d80?w=400&h=400&fit=crop&auto=format&q=80","alt":"Sara Osei","className":"w-8 h-8 rounded-full object-cover object-top"}],["$","div",null,{"children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"font-semibold text-stone-700 dark:text-stone-200 text-sm group-hover:text-orange-600 transition-colors","children":"Sara Osei"}],["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xs text-stone-400","children":"Productivity Researcher"}]]}]]}],["$","span",null,{"className":"flex items-center gap-1","children":[["$","$L4",null,{"iconNode":[["circle",{"cx":"12","cy":"12","r":"10","key":"1mglay"}],["path",{"d":"M12 6v6l4 2","key":"mmk7yg"}]],"className":"lucide-clock","size":13}]," ","8 min read"]}],["$","span",null,{"children":"January 10, 2026"}]]}]]}]
9:["$","div",null,{"className":"grid grid-cols-1 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-10 pb-16","children":[["$","article",null,{"className":"lg:col-span-2","children":[["$","p",null,{"className":"text-xl text-stone-600 leading-relaxed mb-10 font-medium pl-4 border-l-4 border-orange-500","children":"The first hour of your workday largely determines its quality. Not because of productivity hacks, but because of how cognitive resources are allocated and depleted. This guide covers what the research says and what actually works."}],["$","div",null,{"className":"prose","children":[["$","h2","0",{"id":"why-mornings-matter-disproportionately","children":"Why Mornings Matter Disproportionately"}],["$","p","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Your brain's capacity for focused, cognitively demanding work is not evenly distributed across the day. Research on circadian rhythms and cognitive performance consistently shows that analytical thinking, creative problem-solving, and sustained concentration peak in the late morning for most people — typically 9–11 AM."}}],["$","p","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"This window is finite. Decision fatigue accumulates over the course of the day. Every choice you make, every mental task you complete, draws down the available cognitive reserve. By mid-afternoon, the quality of your thinking has measurably declined."}}],["$","p","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The implication: how you spend the first hours of the workday determines what quality of thinking is available for the rest of it."}}],["$","h2","4",{"id":"what-not-to-do-first","children":"What Not to Do First"}],["$","h3","5",{"id":"do-not-check-email-or-slack","children":"Do Not Check Email or Slack"}],["$","p","6",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Email and Slack are priority systems designed by other people. Opening them first thing hands control of your attention to whoever happened to send you something overnight."}}],["$","p","7",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Research by Gloria Mark at UC Irvine found that it takes an average of 23 minutes to return to full concentration after an interruption. Beginning the day by inviting multiple interruptions ensures your peak cognitive window is fragmented before you have used it."}}],["$","p","8",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The rule: do not open communication tools until you have completed at least one significant unit of your own work."}}],["$","h3","9",{"id":"do-not-begin-without-a-plan","children":"Do Not Begin Without a Plan"}],["$","p","10",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Starting the day without knowing your most important task results in defaulting to easy, reactive work — email, minor tasks, administrative work. These feel productive but rarely are."}}],["$","p","11",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Decide the night before, not the morning of. A 10-minute planning session at the end of the previous workday eliminates the morning decision and removes the activation energy barrier to starting."}}],["$","h2","12",{"id":"the-evidence-based-morning-structure","children":"The Evidence-Based Morning Structure"}],["$","h3","13",{"id":"before-sitting-down-physical-activation","children":"Before Sitting Down: Physical Activation"}],["$","p","14",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Light exercise in the morning increases BDNF (brain-derived neurotrophic factor), improves mood, and improves sustained attention. This does not require a full gym session."}}],["$","p","15",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Research supports as little as a 10-minute walk improving subsequent cognitive performance. The mechanism is increased cerebral blood flow and norepinephrine release — both associated with sharper attention."}}],"$Lc","$Ld","$Le","$Lf","$L10","$L11","$L12","$L13","$L14","$L15","$L16","$L17","$L18","$L19","$L1a","$L1b","$L1c","$L1d","$L1e","$L1f","$L20"]}],"$L21","$L22","$L23"]}],"$L24"]}]
a:["$","script","script-0",{"src":"/_next/static/chunks/0wu484j-81s3l.js","async":true}]
b:["$","$L25",null,{"children":["$","$26",null,{"name":"Next.MetadataOutlet","children":"$@27"}]}]
c:["$","p","16",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Practical minimum</strong>: A 15-minute walk before starting work."}}]
d:["$","h3","17",{"id":"the-first-90-minutes-protect-them","children":"The First 90 Minutes: Protect Them"}]
e:["$","p","18",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Your first 90 minutes of peak cognitive state should be reserved for your single most important task. This is not a preference — it is a strategy based on the finite nature of high-quality cognitive resource."}}]
f:["$","p","19",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"What counts as an important task:"}}]
10:["$","ul","20",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The project that will have the most impact this week"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The deliverable you have been avoiding due to its difficulty"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The work that requires your best thinking, not just your availability"}}]]}]
11:["$","p","21",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"What does not count: email, scheduling, administrative work, easy tasks you could do at any energy level."}}]
12:["$","h3","22",{"id":"transition-rituals","children":"Transition Rituals"}]
13:["$","p","23",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A brief ritual at the start of the workday signals to your brain that a different mode is beginning. This is particularly important for remote workers who lack the transition of a commute."}}]
14:["$","p","24",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Simple options:"}}]
15:["$","ul","25",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Reviewing the three tasks you planned the evening before"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Making a cup of coffee or tea before sitting at the desk"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"A five-minute review of the previous day's work to reload context"}}]]}]
16:["$","p","26",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"The content matters less than the consistency. Any brief, repeatable sequence works."}}]
17:["$","h2","27",{"id":"the-practical-morning-structure","children":"The Practical Morning Structure"}]
18:["$","p","28",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>The night before:</strong>"}}]
19:["$","ul","29",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Write three priorities for tomorrow"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Leave the most important task open and visible on your computer"}}]]}]
1a:["$","p","30",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Morning (first 90 minutes):</strong>"}}]
1b:["$","ul","31",{"children":[["$","li","0",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"15-minute walk or brief physical activity"}}],["$","li","1",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Review your three priorities"}}],["$","li","2",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Work on the most important task until the first natural break point"}}],["$","li","3",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"Only then: check email and communication tools"}}]]}]
1c:["$","h2","32",{"id":"what-to-ignore","children":"What to Ignore"}]
1d:["$","p","33",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Complex morning routines</strong>: The research supports physical activity and avoiding reactive communication first thing. Everything else is personal preference."}}]
1e:["$","p","34",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"<strong>Forcing early hours</strong>: Chronotype is real and partially genetic. Find your peak window and protect it — regardless of what time it occurs."}}]
1f:["$","blockquote","35",{"children":"The most productive mornings are not the busiest ones. They are the ones where you do the most important thing first."}]
20:["$","p","36",{"dangerouslySetInnerHTML":{"__html":"For structuring the rest of your workday effectively, read our guide on <a href=\"/blog/time-blocking-guide\" class=\"text-orange-600 dark:text-orange-400 hover:underline font-medium\">time blocking</a>."}}]
